root_cause: Execution gap, not message quality failure. All 40 DMs were drafted and Codex-reviewed across 7 days. Only the 7 day-1 contacts were sent (IDs 1, 4, 5, 13, 25, 32, 40). Days 2-7 produced strong, personalized drafts with specific hooks but the founder did not send them. Follow-ups for day-1 contacts were also due 2026-07-02 and were not sent.

kill_criteria_assessment.reason: Contract kill criteria require >= 40 contacts to evaluate. Only 7 were sent. The 0-of-7 reply rate is a weak signal — the day-1 ask was discovery-only ('how are you thinking about authorization?') with no explicit CTA. Reply rate for that ask type is typically low without a warm prior relationship. Cannot distinguish message failure from execution gap with this sample size.
kill_criteria_assessment.message_quality: Unresolved. The 33 unsent drafts have not been tested at all. The 7 sent drafts targeted: 2 cold DevRel (Harish, jordo1138), 2 cold teams (Firecrawl, Questflow), 2 follow-ups on stale DMs from June 13 (Peter Steinberger, Thariq), and 1 warm (Kevin Leffew). Zero replies from 7 cold/stale contacts is not surprising; Kevin Leffew is the one warm contact in the batch and a follow-up DM is now 4 days overdue.

day_2.notes: x402_infra builders: AltLayer, Daydreams, PayAI, Bank of AI, xpay, nova402, GaiaNet/second-state, Blockscout. All first-contact, strong hooks.

day_3.notes: agent_framework + ecosystem: Crossmint, Liam Horne (MPP), Hyperbolic, Zuplo (Josh Twist), Zuplo (Nate Totten), Shahzad Safri, Matt Wright (GaiaNet), Amir Sarhangi (Skyfire). All first-contact.

day_4.notes: Mix: Must-be-Ash, agenthustler, xpay, Alfonso (Crossmint), Josh Twist, Nate Totten, Shahzad Safri, Matt Wright. De-dup with day 3 before sending (IDs 17, 22, 26, 27, 29, 33 overlap).

day_5.notes: Skyfire CEO, Hyperbolic, Shahzad Safri, Craig DeWitt (Skyfire CPO), Jeremy Allaire (Circle), Matt Wright, World AgentKit, Solana hackathon. De-dup with days 3-4.

day_6.notes: Kite AI (competitor), Craig DeWitt, Jeremy Allaire, Kevin Leffew follow-up, World, Blockscout agent skills, Solana hackathon template (unactionable without name), Berlin hackathon template (unactionable without name). IDs 38 and 39 require name identification before sending.

day_7.notes: Best batch in the loop. Nathan Schwermann (hackathon winner, ICP), Rodrigo Coelho (ampersend), David Tso (warm, Base), Merit Systems (x402scan), Joao Moura (CrewAI PR #6382 leverage), Liam Horne (MPP), Eric Ciarla (Firecrawl co-founder, PR #3902), Brett Calhoun (investor, warm).

top_priority_unsent.name: Joao Moura
top_priority_unsent.reason: PR #6382 open on CrewAI repo — only prospect with a concrete code artifact waiting for a decision. Reply here converts fastest to proof point.

top_priority_unsent.name: Nathan Schwermann
top_priority_unsent.reason: Won Cronos x402 Hackathon with permission-scoping implementation — closest ICP match in the pipeline.

top_priority_unsent.name: David Tso
top_priority_unsent.reason: Warmest unconverted contact (replied to pinned tweet organically). A single reply produces ecosystem intros, not just one conversation.

top_priority_unsent.name: Kevin Leffew
top_priority_unsent.reason: Warmest prospect in day-1 batch — 2x liked posts, follow-up DM is now 4 days overdue. Day-6 follow-up draft is ready.

top_priority_unsent.name: Rodrigo Coelho
top_priority_unsent.reason: ampersend is the management layer for x402 payments — authorization signal lives here. Integration would propagate to every flow running through ampersend.

top_priority_unsent.name: Alfonso Gomez-Jordana
top_priority_unsent.reason: Publicly named authorization as the gap across all agent payment protocols (ACP, AP2, x402). Has already done the analysis that confirms the problem.

top_priority_unsent.name: Merit Systems
top_priority_unsent.reason: Coinbase-endorsed x402 explorer — if authorization signals appear in x402 transactions, x402scan is where they'd surface. Infrastructure integration produces the most durable proof point.

top_priority_unsent.name: Jia Yaoqi
top_priority_unsent.reason: x402-as-a-Service with $14.4M raised. Authorization middleware plugs directly into their facilitator.

recommendations.primary: Run a 5-day 'flush' loop starting immediately. All 40 DMs are written and reviewed. The loop did not fail at message quality — it failed at execution. De-dup the day 3-6 overlap targets before sending.
recommendations.channel: For GitHub-channel prospects (IDs 5, 8, 17, 18, 19), route to GitHub Issues or PR comments — discovery rate is higher and the conversation is asynchronous with lower friction.
recommendations.message_wedge: Do NOT change the wedge based on current data. The 33 unsent drafts from days 2-7 are progressively more specific and better calibrated. The wedge was never tested at scale.
recommendations.immediate_action: Kevin Leffew (ID 32) follow-up is 4 days overdue and has the warmest pre-existing signal. Send the day-6 draft today before the signal cools.
recommendations.kill_criteria_trigger: If after flushing remaining 33 drafts and all follow-ups: < 3 positive replies from 40 contacts → kill the message frame (pure discovery question), test the 20-min test offer ('I'll run a test against your endpoint') as the primary ask instead.
recommendations.pr_leverage: PR #6382 (CrewAI) and PR #3902 (Firecrawl) are open. Do not merge, bump, or close PRs while outreach to Joao Moura (ID 21) and Eric Ciarla (ID 28) is pending.

loop_verdict: The loop structure, ICP targeting, and message quality are all sound. The loop did not produce a design partner because 83% of drafted messages were never sent. The distribution problem is a founder throughput problem, not a product-market signal problem. Recommendation: restart execution, not strategy.
